mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month change in industrial production signals that firms are rebuilding output and revenue expectations, causing S&P 500 forward 20-day returns to be positive when the production trend is rising.
failure_condition: Reject if rising 3-month INDPRO changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of observations or if the walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment greater than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months identifies an adverse labor-market shock that reduces expected household income and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered cases or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above 4.5% and CPI is still rising over 3 months, restrictive real-rate pressure and persistent inflation lower equity valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: Reject if high-FEDFUNDS and rising-CPI triggered observations produce nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if the interaction term has the wrong sign in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in retail sales indicates stronger final demand and near-term revenue momentum, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when consumption data are improving.
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS upturn triggers do not deliver higher mean 20-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered observations or if the hit rate is below 52% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts greater than 10% over 3 months signals weakening credit-sensitive investment and future construction activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if housing-start decline triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if triggered average returns exceed unconditional average returns.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment rises over 3 months while unemployment falls, broad labor income is improving and recession risk is declining, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down state has no positive excess 20-day return relative to baseline or if either single proxy fully subsumes the interaction in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate represents monetary easing that lowers discount rates and supports risk appetite, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days after the easing signal appears.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS decline triggers produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of cases or if triggered average 5-day returns are not above the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth is positive and industrial production also rises, the economy is expanding across final demand and output, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter change > 0 and INDPRO 3-month change > 0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive-GDPC1 and positive-INDPRO state fails to produce positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if walk-forward hit rates fall below 52%.
